trackingsummarydata.subject: This week in A&S: Kudos to our faculty award winners
trackingsummarydata.title: FSSG - 10/20/25

urls.page_summary: Awards and honors received by faculty, postdocs and graduate students in the College of Arts & Sciences.
urls.page_title: Fuchs, Hartman elected to American Physical Society | Cornell Chronicle
urls.page_summary: Greg Fuchs, the James R. Meehl Professor in School of Applied and Engineering Physics, and Thomas Hartman, professor in the Department of Physics in the College of Arts and Sciences, have been elected as fellows of the American Physical Society.
urls.page_title: Bulawayo wins 25th anniversary Best of Caine prize
urls.page_summary: NoViolet Bulawayo, M.F.A. ’10, assistant professor in A&S, has won the Best of Caine Award as judges have chosen her short story, “Hitting Budapest,” as the best to have won the Caine Prize for African Writing in the award’s 25 years.
urls.page_title: Zhao wins Simons Foundation Fellows-to-Faculty award
urls.page_summary: Zhao researches the neural mechanisms of parrot vocalizations in the Goldberg lab.
urls.page_title: The Phantom of the Opera: Live score by Invincible Czars
urls.page_summary: This October, Cornell Cinema will celebrate the 100th anniversary of the silent movie “The Phantom of the Opera,” with live musical accompaniment by The Invincible Czars.
urls.page_title: Neuroscientist Azahara Oliva receives Packard Fellowship
urls.page_summary: The unrestricted fellowship funds enable Oliva and the 19 other fellows named this year to “test novel ideas and lead research that drives real-world impact.”
urls.page_title: Cornell composer’s work featured in upcoming Dallas Morse Coors Concert Series event
urls.page_summary: A new work by Cornell alum Zachary Wadsworth DMA ’12, will premiere this weekend in three concerts, including one at Cornell’s Bailey Hall.
